model_output_raw_text: {"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 3e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 5e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"Lower the base learning rate now that warmup and cosine decay are in place; this should make the AdamW run less aggressive and often improves final validation loss in short fixed-step training.","secondary_modes":["summaries"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 5e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 3e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.99)","old":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.999)","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"Restore the stronger learning-rate scale from the prior better run and make AdamW more responsive by lowering beta2, which is often better for short fixed-step optimization.","secondary_modes":["indexing"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 3e-5","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"Relax AdamW regularization a bit; with the current short fixed-step regime, the model may still be slightly over-regularized and a smaller decay can improve final validation loss.","secondary_modes":["indexing"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 256","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 160","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 3e-5","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"Restore the stronger regularization point from the best run and widen the classifier bottleneck a bit; the current dense head may be slightly underpowered relative to the convolutional features.","secondary_modes":["caching"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 384","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 256","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The wider classifier head helped, so the next low-risk move is to widen it a bit more; the current flattened feature vector still has room for a larger bottleneck before the final logits.","secondary_modes":[]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 32","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 28","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 256","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 384","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The wider classifier was too aggressive, so move that capacity back into the convolutional stem while restoring the smaller 256-unit head that was previously better balanced.","secondary_modes":[]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 5e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 4e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"Nudge the AdamW step size down slightly from the current best setting to see whether a bit more stability improves final validation loss under the same fixed-step budget.","secondary_modes":["summaries"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 4e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 4.5e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"The current best appears to be near the top end of the useful learning-rate range, so a small upward nudge may improve optimization without destabilizing the run.","secondary_modes":["summaries"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 4.5e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 4.2e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"The previous 4.5e-3 step moved away from the best run, so a modest reduction is the most direct follow-up to probe the local optimum around 4e-3 without changing the model or schedule.","secondary_modes":["summaries"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"DEPTH = 3","new":"DEPTH = 4","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The current 3-block CNN may be hitting a feature-extraction ceiling; adding one more pooled conv block is the most direct structural test for improving representation quality without changing the optimizer regime.","secondary_modes":[]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 32","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 40","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The added depth helped substantially, so the next low-risk move is to give each stage a bit more width and see whether the deeper feature stack can use the extra capacity.","secondary_modes":[]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 256","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 384","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The deeper and wider feature extractor is now in place, so the next likely bottleneck is the 256-unit classifier head; widening it is the cheapest way to test for more representational capacity.","secondary_modes":[]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 40","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 48","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 384","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 256","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The wider head overshot, so restore the smaller 256-unit classifier and shift the capacity back into the convolutional stem with one more width step.","secondary_modes":[]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 48","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 56","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The deeper CNN has responded well to more stem width, so a moderate bump from 48 to 56 channels is the next simple capacity test without making the model disproportionately large.","secondary_modes":[]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 56","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 48","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The last width increase regressed validation loss, and 48 channels was the best seen configuration, so reverting to that proven stem width is the safest next move.","secondary_modes":[]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 4.2e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 4.1e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"The current best is already close to the apparent optimum, so a small learning-rate reduction is the most conservative way to probe for a tiny stability gain without changing the architecture that already worked best.","secondary_modes":["summaries"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 4.1e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 4e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"The current run improved when the learning rate moved down slightly, so one more small step toward 4e-3 is the most direct remaining probe of the local optimum.","secondary_modes":["summaries"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 4e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 4.1e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"The last downward nudge regressed, so the safest final move is to restore the prior 4.1e-3 setting that produced the best loss seen in this run.","secondary_modes":["summaries"]}
